g.data-narration: Environmental pollution is one of humanity's greatest challenges. Let's explore its sources, impacts, and solutions.
g.text: Sources, Impacts, Preventive Measures & Public Health

g.data-narration: Pollution is the introduction of harmful substances into our environment, affecting health, wildlife, and quality of life globally.
g.text: Introduction of harmful substances into the natural environment
causing adverse effects on living organisms and ecosystems.

g.data-narration: We'll study four main topics: types of pollution sources, environmental impacts, health effects, and preventive solutions.
Air pollution has primary sources that emit directly, and secondary sources that create pollutants through atmospheric chemical reactions.
Water pollution comes from point sources like factory pipes, and non-point sources like agricultural runoff carrying fertilizers.
Soil pollution results from industrial waste disposal, agricultural chemicals, and improper handling of hazardous materials.
g.text: Industrial waste • Agricultural pesticides/fertilizers • Hazardous materials

g.data-narration: Key air pollutants include PM2.5 and PM10 particulates, sulfur dioxide, nitrogen oxides, ozone, and carbon monoxide.
Air pollution causes global warming, acid rain, ozone depletion, and smog formation, affecting climate and ecosystems worldwide.
Water pollution leads to eutrophication, bioaccumulation of toxins, and groundwater contamination affecting drinking water.
WHO estimates 92 percent of the global population lives in areas exceeding air quality guidelines, with 7 million premature deaths annually.
Airborne particulates PM2.5 and PM10 penetrate deep into lungs, causing asthma, bronchitis, emphysema, and lung cancer.
g.text: PM2.5, PM10, O3, and NO2 penetrate deep into lungs causing:

g.data-narration: Air pollution causes cardiovascular problems through inflammation and oxidative stress, plus waterborne diseases and cancer risks.
The Air Quality Index combines PM2.5, PM10, O3, NO2, CO, and SO2 to provide a simple measure of air quality status.
g.text: AQI = f(PM2.5, PM10, O3, NO2, CO, SO2)

g.data-narration: BOD measures oxygen needed by microorganisms to decompose organic matter, while COD measures total chemical oxygen demand.
g.text: O2 required to decompose organic matter
Total O2 required for chemical oxidation

g.data-narration: At individual level: use public transport, recycle, conserve energy and water, and avoid single-use plastics.
Industries should implement green manufacturing, install filtration systems, treat wastewater, and adopt circular economy principles.
Governments should enforce regulations, promote renewables, invest in public transport, and establish monitoring systems.
Three major case studies: Delhi air crisis, Minamata disease from mercury, and Bhopal gas tragedy demonstrating pollution devastating effects.
Key Indian laws include Air Act 1981, Water Act 1974, and Environment Protection Act 1986. International: Paris Agreement, Montreal Protocol.
As CSE students, relate to IoT sensors for monitoring, data analytics for modeling, smart traffic systems, and sustainable software development.
g.text: TECH CONNECTION - Green Computing for CSE
